Our compact diamond quantum magnetometers provide high-sensitivity, vector magnetic sensing to detect subtle magnetic changes with low noise and minimal drift, while operating passively in ambient conditions.

Quantum computers usually mean massive machines, cryogenic temperatures, and isolated data centers. But what if quantum computing could run at room temperature, fit inside a server rack — or even a satellite? In this episode of TechFirst, host John Koetsier sits down with Marcus Doherty to explore how diamond-based quantum computers work — and why they could unlock scalable, edge-deployed quantum systems.
